title: Preparation of Chromatin Fragments From Human Cells for Cryo-EM Analysis
author: Suguru Hatazawa
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: HeLa S3 cells
cryogenic electron microscope
NE-PER nuclear and cytoplasmic extraction reagents (Thermo Fisher Scientific, catalog number: 78833 or
Buffer A (see Recipes)
Proteinase K mix (see Recipes)
10% sucrose gradient solution (see Recipes)
50% sucrose gradient solution (see Recipes)
Buffer B (see Recipes) Recipes

Prepare MNase solutions at the desired concentrations by performing serial dilutions. Example: Prepare MNase solutions at 200, 40, 8, 1.6, and 0.32 U/µL. Note: Dilute MNase using buffer A, since diluting MNase in Milli-Q water may reduce its enzymatic activity. These MNase concentrations are provided as a reference. Higher concentrations can also be prepared if required.
HeLa S3 cells (ATCC, CCL-2.
Dulbecco’s modified Eagle medium (DMEM) (Nacalai, catalog number: 08458-
Fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco, catalog number: 26140-
Penicillin-streptomycin mixed solution (Nacalai, catalog number: 09367-
Dulbecco’s phosphate-buffered saline, liquid [D-PBS(-)] without Ca and Mg (Nacalai, catalog number: 14249-
2.5 g/L Trypsin/1 mmol/L EDTA solution (Nacalai, catalog number: 35554-64) (used for cell passaging)
Protease inhibitor cocktail (EDTA-free) (100×) (Nacalai, catalog number: 03969-
Formaldehyde solution (36%–38%) (Nacalai, catalog number: 16223-
Micrococcal nuclease (MNase) (New England Biolabs, catalog number: M0247S)
Proteinase K, recombinant, PCR grade (Merck, catalog number:
Agarose for 50~800 bp fragment (Nacalai, catalog number: 01147-
100 bp DNA ladder (TAKARA, catalog number: 3407A)
Ethidium bromide solution (10 mg/mL) (EtBr) (Nacalai, catalog number: 14631-
2 M glycine (prepared from glycine powder) (Nacalai, catalog number: 17109-
1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) (prepared from Tris base) (Nacalai, catalog number: 35434-
1 M Tris-HCl (pH 7.5) (prepared from Tris base) (Nacalai, catalog number: 35434-
1 M HEPES-NaOH (pH 7.5) (prepared from HEPES) (Nacalai, catalog number: 17514-
4 M KCl (prepared from potassium chloride) (Nacalai, catalog number: 28514-
5 M NaCl (prepared from sodium chloride) (Nacalai, catalog number: 31320-
1 M CaCl 2 (prepared from calcium chloride) (Nacalai, catalog number: 08894-
1 M DTT (prepared from dithiothreitol powder) (Nacalai, catalog number: 14128-

steps.title: Milli-Q water (used directly from the...
steps.instruction: Milli-Q water (used directly from the purification system without additional filtration)

steps.title: 1× TAE (prepared from Tris base,...
steps.instruction: 1× TAE (prepared from Tris base, acetic acid, and EDTA) Solutions

steps.title: Buffer A Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Buffer A Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.

steps.title: 10 mM 500 μL 4 M KCl 200 mM 2
steps.instruction: 10 mM 500 μL 4 M KCl 200 mM 2.5 mL 1 M CaCl 2 1 mM 50 μL 10% NP-40 0.5% 2.5 mL Milli-Q water n/a up to 50 mL Total n/a 50 mL Used for chromatin fragmentation by MNase. Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Proteinase K mix Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Proteinase K mix Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume Proteinase K 1/2 of the stock 30 μL 10% SDS (w/v) 1.67% 10 μL Milli-Q water n/a 20 μL Total n/a 60 μL Used for digesting proteins in chromatin fragments to estimate DNA size by agarose gel electrophoresis.

steps.title: 10% sucrose gradient solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 10% sucrose gradient solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 1 M HEPES-NaOH (pH 7.

steps.title: 10 mM 500 μL 5 M...
steps.instruction: 10 mM 500 μL 5 M NaCl 30 mM 300 μL 1 M DTT 1 mM 50 μL Sucrose 10% (w/v) 5 g Milli-Q water n/a up to 50 mL Total n/a 50 mL Add DTT immediately before use. The lower-density sucrose solution is used for ultracentrifugation.

steps.title: 50% sucrose gradient solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 50% sucrose gradient solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 1 M HEPES-NaOH (pH 7.

steps.title: 10 mM 500 μL 5 M...
steps.instruction: 10 mM 500 μL 5 M NaCl 30 mM 300 μL 1 M DTT 1 mM 50 μL Sucrose 50% (w/v) 25 g Milli-Q water n/a up to 50 mL Total n/a 50 mL Add DTT immediately before use. The higher-density sucrose solution is used for ultracentrifugation.

steps.title: Buffer B Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Buffer B Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 7.

steps.title: 10 mM 5 mL 5 M...
steps.instruction: 10 mM 5 mL 5 M NaCl 30 mM 3 mL 1 M DTT 1 mM 500 μL Milli-Q water n/a up to 500 mL Total n/a 500 mL Keep chilled at 4 °C and add DTT immediately before use. Used for buffer exchange after ultracentrifugation prior to cryo-EM sample preparation. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Seed HeLa S3 cells in 10...
steps.instruction: Seed HeLa S3 cells in 10 cm culture dishes in 10 mL of DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and penicillin-streptomycin (1.0 × 10 6 cells/dish).

steps.title: Culture the cells at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Culture the cells at 37 °C in a humidified atmosphere containing 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: When the cells reach a subconfluent...
steps.instruction: When the cells reach a subconfluent state (approximately 70%–80% confluency), repeat passaging of the cells. Note: A total of 2 × 10 7 cells (~4 dishes) is the minimum amount required for cryo-EM analysis.

steps.title: Aspirate the medium and wash the...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the medium and wash the cells twice with 5 mL of PBS prechilled at 4 °C.

steps.title: Harvest the subconfluent cells with a cell scraper
steps.instruction: Harvest the subconfluent cells with a cell scraper.

steps.title: Resuspend the harvested cells in 10...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the harvested cells in 10 mL of cold PBS and transfer the suspension to a 15 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Count the cells using an automated...
steps.instruction: Count the cells using an automated cell counter or a hemocytometer.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 500× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 500× g for 5 min at 4 °C and discard the supernatant using a pipette.

steps.title: Place the tube on ice
steps.instruction: Place the tube on ice. B. Nuclei isolation, crosslinking, and sonication

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet in cold...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet in cold CERI buffer (from NE-PER nuclear and cytoplasmic extraction reagents) to a concentration of 1.0 × 10 7 cells/mL. Note: Nuclei isolation using CERI and CERII buffers was performed entirely according to the manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: Add 1/100 volume of protease inhibitor cocktail
steps.instruction: Add 1/100 volume of protease inhibitor cocktail.

steps.title: Vortex the tube for 15 s...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tube for 15 s and place it on ice for 10 min.

steps.title: Add 55 μL of cold CERII...
steps.instruction: Add 55 μL of cold CERII buffer (from NE-PER nuclear and cytoplasmic extraction reagents) per 1 mL of sample, then vortex for 5 s.

steps.title: Place the tube on ice for...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on ice for 1 min, then vortex for 5 s.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and discard the supernatant using a pipette.

steps.title: Resuspend the nuclear pellet in cold...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the nuclear pellet in cold PBS to a concentration of 1.0 × 10 7 nuclei/mL, calculated from the initial cell number.

steps.title: Dispense 500 μL of the sample into tubes (0
steps.instruction: Dispense 500 μL of the sample into tubes (0.5 × 10 7 nuclei/tube).

steps.title: Add formaldehyde to a final concentration...
steps.instruction: Add formaldehyde to a final concentration of 1% and mix by pipetting and inverting the tube.

steps.title: Incubate the sample for 5 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample for 5 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 2 M glycine to a...
steps.instruction: Add 2 M glycine to a final concentration of 150 mM and mix by pipetting and inverting the tube.

steps.title: Incubate the sample for 3 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample for 3 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and discard the supernatant using a pipette.

steps.title: Resuspend the nuclear pellet in buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the nuclear pellet in buffer A [10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0), 200 mM KCl, 1 mM CaCl 2 , 0.5% NP-40] to a concentration of 2.0 × 10 7 nuclei/500 μL (4.0 × 10 7 nuclei/mL) and place it on ice. Note: The nuclear pellet does not need to be fully resuspended, as it tends to be very sticky. However, it is advisable to resuspend it as much as possible by pipetting and during the subsequent sonication step (vortexing is ineffective for resuspension).

steps.title: Dispense 500 μL aliquots of the...
steps.instruction: Dispense 500 μL aliquots of the sample into tubes and keep them on ice.

steps.title: Sonicate the nuclear suspension using a...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the nuclear suspension using a VP-050N ultrasonic homogenizer (TAITEC) equipped with a microtip (2 mm in diameter) under the following conditions: PWM = 20%, 3 s on/7 s off, for 6 cycles ( Figure 1A ).

steps.title: Place the sample on ice
steps.instruction: Place the sample on ice. Figure

steps.title: Sonication of nuclear suspension and MNase titration for chromatin fragmentation
steps.instruction: Sonication of nuclear suspension and MNase titration for chromatin fragmentation. (A) Photographs showing the sonication process used for nuclear suspension. (B) Agarose gel electrophoresis of chromatin fragmented by varying units of MNase. A 10 μL portion of each sample with (+) or without (–) proteinase K treatment was applied to the agarose gel, electrophoresed in 1× TAE buffer, and stained with EtBr. The extent of chromatin fragmentation by MNase was estimated based on the nucleosomal DNA bands observed in the proteinase K-treated sample (+). C. Titration of MNase for chromatin fragmentation

steps.title: Collect a portion of the sample...
steps.instruction: Collect a portion of the sample and treat it with MNase solution. Example: Collect 10 μL of the sample into each of five tubes and mix the sample with 0.5 μL of MNase at the indicated concentrations by pipetting.

steps.title: Incubate the samples for 40 min at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples for 40 min at 37 °C.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 M EDTA to a final concentration of 10 mM.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and collect the supernatants.

steps.title: Place the tubes on ice
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on ice.

steps.title: Collect a portion of the MNase-treated...
steps.instruction: Collect a portion of the MNase-treated samples and add the proteinase K mix, then incubate the sample for 15 min at 37 °C. Example: From the 10 μL MNase-treated sample, 5 μL was mixed with 0.5 μL of the proteinase K mix. The remaining 5 μL was used directly for gel electrophoresis. Note: Proteinase K remains active under the 10 mM EDTA conditions used for quenching the MNase digestion.

steps.title: Add 30% (w/v) sucrose to each...
steps.instruction: Add 30% (w/v) sucrose to each sample (with or without proteinase K treatment) to a final concentration of 5% (w/v). Example: Add 1 μL of 30% sucrose to 5 μL of each sample.

steps.title: Apply the samples to a 1
steps.instruction: Apply the samples to a 1.5% agarose gel and perform electrophoresis in 1× TAE buffer.

steps.title: Soak the gel in EtBr (0
steps.instruction: Soak the gel in EtBr (0.5 µg/mL) and gently shake for 10 min.

steps.title: Transfer the gel to Milli-Q water...
steps.instruction: Transfer the gel to Milli-Q water and gently shake for 10 min.

steps.title: Image the gel using a UV...
steps.instruction: Image the gel using a UV transilluminator ( Figure 1B ). Note: Determine the appropriate MNase concentration for chromatin fragmentation by evaluating the DNA band pattern of proteinase K-treated samples. D. Chromatin fragmentation by MNase and ultracentrifugation

steps.title: Prepare the gradient solution [10%–50% (w/v)...
steps.instruction: Prepare the gradient solution [10%–50% (w/v) sucrose, 10 mM HEPES-NaOH (pH 7.5), 30 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT] using a Gradient Master 108 at room temperature in ultra-clear tubes (14 × 89 mm).

steps.title: Chill the prepared gradient at 4 °C prior to ultracentrifugation
steps.instruction: Chill the prepared gradient at 4 °C prior to ultracentrifugation.

steps.title: Pre-incubate the remaining sample for 5...
steps.instruction: Pre-incubate the remaining sample for 5 min at 37 °C prior to MNase treatment.

steps.title: Add MNase to the remaining sample,...
steps.instruction: Add MNase to the remaining sample, based on the conditions determined in the titration experiment.

steps.title: Incubate the sample for 40 min at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample for 40 min at 37 °C.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 M EDTA to a final concentration of 10 mM.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C and collect the supernatant (~500 μL) using a pipette without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Apply the fragmented chromatin onto the...
steps.instruction: Apply the fragmented chromatin onto the top of the gradient solution ( Figure 2A ). Note: As a control for the fractionation step, reserve a portion of the fragmented chromatin sample (as input) prior to centrifugation and store it on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge at ~210,000× g (35,000 rpm,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at ~210,000× g (35,000 rpm, using an SW 41 Ti rotor) for 21 h at 4 °C, using an Optima XPN-80 ultracentrifuge. Figure

steps.title: Separation of chromatin fragments by ultracentrifugation
steps.instruction: Separation of chromatin fragments by ultracentrifugation. (A) Photograph of sample application onto the top of the gradient solution. (B) Fractionation of chromatin fragments from the gradient solution after ultracentrifugation. (C) Agarose gel electrophoresis of chromatin fractions obtained by sucrose density gradient ultracentrifugation. Chromatin was fragmented with 4 units of MNase per 10 μL of sample and then subjected to ultracentrifugation. A 20 μL portion of each fraction was applied to the agarose gel, electrophoresed in 1× TAE buffer, and stained with EtBr. Fractions enclosed by magenta dashed boxes were combined, subjected to buffer exchange, and subsequently concentrated. E. Fractionation, buffer exchange, and concentration of chromatin fragments

steps.title: Fractionate the solution by collecting 660...
steps.instruction: Fractionate the solution by collecting 660 μL aliquots from the top of the gradient and place them on ice ( Figure 2B ).

steps.title: Collect portions of the fractionated samples,...
steps.instruction: Collect portions of the fractionated samples, add the proteinase K mix, and incubate for 15 min at 37 °C. Example: Collect 20 μL of the fraction and mix with 2 μL of the proteinase K mix by pipetting. For the input sample, mix 5 μL of the input with 0.5 μL of the proteinase K mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Apply the proteinase K-treated fractions to a 1
steps.instruction: Apply the proteinase K-treated fractions to a 1.5% agarose gel and perform electrophoresis in 1× TAE buffer.

steps.title: Soak the gel in EtBr and...
steps.instruction: Soak the gel in EtBr and gently shake for 10 min.

steps.title: Transfer the gel to Milli-Q water...
steps.instruction: Transfer the gel to Milli-Q water and gently shake for 10 min.

steps.title: Image the gel using a UV...
steps.instruction: Image the gel using a UV transilluminator ( Figure 2C ).

steps.title: Combine the fractions that contain similar...
steps.instruction: Combine the fractions that contain similar DNA fragment lengths, as estimated from the proteinase K-treated samples.

steps.title: Exchange the buffer of the combined...
steps.instruction: Exchange the buffer of the combined fractions using a PD-10 column (Cytiva) to Buffer B [10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 30 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT].

steps.title: Concentrate the fractions with an Amicon...
steps.instruction: Concentrate the fractions with an Amicon Ultra-4 centrifugal filter unit (30,000 MWCO).

steps.title: Measure the dsDNA concentration of fractions...
steps.instruction: Measure the dsDNA concentration of fractions with a NanoDrop One spectrophotometer. F. Grid preparation of chromatin fragments

steps.title: Hydrophilize Quantifoil R1
steps.instruction: Hydrophilize Quantifoil R1.2/1.3 200-mesh Cu grids by glow discharge, using a PIB-10 set to soft mode with a 2 min timer.

steps.title: Perform the following steps using a...
steps.instruction: Perform the following steps using a Vitrobot Mark IV at 4 °C under 100% humidity.

steps.title: Apply 2
steps.instruction: Apply 2.5 μL of the samples onto the glow-discharged grids.

steps.title: Blot the samples for 4–6 s (wait time
steps.instruction: Blot the samples for 4–6 s (wait time: 0 s; blot force: 0; blot total: 1; drain time: 0 s).

steps.title: Plunge the grids into liquid ethane...
steps.instruction: Plunge the grids into liquid ethane and mount them into a C-clip and C-clip ring.

steps.title: Store the grids in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Store the grids in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Acquire micrographs using cryo-EM ( Figure 3 )
steps.instruction: Acquire micrographs using cryo-EM ( Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: Micrographs of chromatin extracted from HeLa cells
steps.instruction: Micrographs of chromatin extracted from HeLa cells. Images were acquired using a Krios G4 cryo-TEM equipped with an energy-filtered K3 detector (Gatan). Data were collected at 300 kV accelerating voltage and 81,000× magnification (corresponding to a pixel size of 1.06 Å), with a defocus setting of -2.5 µm. Each micrograph was generated from 40 movie frames acquired in the electron counting mode over a total exposure time of 4.5 s, resulting in a total dose of ~60 e-/Å 2 . Scale bars represent 100 nm. Representative mono-, di-, and poly-nucleosomes are enclosed by white dotted lines.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol is an optimized version of the chromatin preparation method for cryo-EM analysis previously described in our original research article: Hatazawa et al. [19]. Cryo‐EM structures of native chromatin units from human cells. Genes to Cells (Figures 1–4 and Figure S1).
